description: Pull content topics from Airtable, generate a draft with Anthropic, and publish to Notion for review.

steps.action: Fetch next scheduled content item from the editorial calendar
steps.error_hint: Ensure the Airtable API key has read access to the content base. Check field names match exactly.

steps.action: Generate article draft based on topic, keywords, and target audience

input_mapping.prompt: Write a blog post about {{step1.topic}} targeting {{step1.target_audience}}. Include keywords: {{step1.keywords}}

steps.error_hint: If content is too long, split into sections. Keep prompt under 2000 tokens for best results.

steps.action: Create a new page in the content review database with the generated draft
steps.error_hint: Notion API has a 2000 block limit per request. Split long content into chunks if needed.

gotchas: Airtable API rate limit is 5 requests per second per base — if the editorial calendar is large and requires pagination, add delays between page fetches or you will get 429 errors with a 30-second backoff.
Anthropic API requires an explicit max_tokens parameter — if omitted, the request fails with a validation error. Set it based on expected article length (4000 tokens is roughly 3000 words).

Notion page content is structured as blocks, not plain text — the AI-generated markdown must be converted to Notion block format (paragraph, heading, bulleted_list_item, etc.) before creation, or the content appears as a single unformatted text blob.

Airtable field names are case-sensitive and space-sensitive — 'publish_date' and 'Publish Date' are different fields. Verify exact field names from the Airtable schema before building the filter formula.

goal: Streamline incident response across tools
description: When PagerDuty triggers an alert, create a Jira ticket, notify the on-call team in Slack, and annotate Grafana.

steps.action: Receive incident trigger with severity, service name, and description
steps.error_hint: Configure PagerDuty webhook to send V3 events format for richer payload

steps.action: Create incident ticket with priority mapped from PagerDuty severity
steps.error_hint: Ensure 'Incident' issue type exists in the Jira project. Map PagerDuty P1-P5 to Jira priority levels.

steps.action: Post incident alert to the on-call channel with Jira link
steps.error_hint: Consider using @channel mention for P1/P2 incidents only to avoid alert fatigue

steps.action: Create annotation on the relevant dashboard to mark incident start time
steps.error_hint: Use Grafana API token with annotation write permissions. Dashboard ID is numeric, not the slug.

gotchas: PagerDuty Events API v2 requires a routing_key (integration key), not the REST API token — using the wrong key type returns a 'routing key not found' error. Create a service-level Events API v2 integration in PagerDuty to get the correct key.

Jira priority names vary by instance — the default is 'Highest/High/Medium/Low/Lowest', not P1-P5. Query /rest/api/3/priority to get your instance's priority IDs before mapping from PagerDuty severity.
Grafana annotation timestamps must be in epoch milliseconds — PagerDuty's triggered_at is ISO 8601. Convert before calling the Grafana API or the annotation will be placed at Unix epoch (1970).
PagerDuty webhook V3 format nests incident data under event.data — V2 uses messages[].incident. If you configured V3 but parse V2 format, all fields will be undefined.

goal: Analyze customer feedback with AI and route insights
description: Collect survey responses from Airtable, run AI sentiment analysis, and post a summary report to Slack.

steps.action: Fetch new unprocessed survey responses from the feedback table
steps.error_hint: Add a 'processed' checkbox field to track which responses have been analyzed

steps.action: Run sentiment analysis and theme extraction on feedback batch

input_mapping.prompt:
Analyze these customer feedback responses. For each, provide sentiment (positive/neutral/negative) and key themes:
{{step1.records}}

steps.error_hint: Batch responses to stay under token limits. Process max 20 responses per API call.

steps.action: Post weekly feedback digest with sentiment breakdown and top themes
steps.error_hint: If analysis is too long for a single message, use Slack thread replies for details

gotchas: OpenAI rate limits on gpt-4o vary by tier — sending a batch of 20 survey responses in a single prompt can exceed token-per-minute limits on lower tiers. Process in smaller batches of 5-10 responses if you hit 429 errors.

Airtable records must be marked as processed after analysis to prevent reprocessing — use a PATCH request to set the 'processed' checkbox immediately after the OpenAI call, not after the Slack post, to avoid reprocessing if Slack fails.
Slack messages over 4000 characters are truncated — sentiment analysis of 20+ responses will likely exceed this. Post a summary in the main message and use a thread for the per-response breakdown.

Airtable's filterByFormula syntax uses its own formula language (not SQL) — the filter 'processed=false' must be written as filterByFormula=NOT({processed}) or {processed}=FALSE() depending on field type.

goal: Process e-commerce orders with inventory tracking
description: When a Shopify order comes in, update Airtable inventory, send confirmation via Resend, and notify operations in Slack.

steps.action: Receive new order webhook with line items, customer info, and totals
steps.error_hint: Verify Shopify webhook HMAC signature. Handle test orders from Shopify Bogus Gateway separately.

steps.action: Decrement inventory quantities for each ordered item
steps.error_hint: Use Airtable's PATCH endpoint for atomic updates. Check for negative inventory values.

steps.action: Send order confirmation email with itemized receipt
steps.error_hint: Set up the order-confirmation template in Resend before first use

steps.action: Notify operations channel with order summary and any low-stock alerts
steps.error_hint: Use conditional formatting to only show low-stock alert when relevant

gotchas: Shopify webhook HMAC signature must be verified before processing. Bogus Gateway test orders should be filtered out to avoid decrementing real inventory in Airtable.
Airtable PATCH endpoint does not support atomic decrement — you must read current quantity, subtract, then write. Under concurrent orders, this creates a race condition. Use a queue or mutex pattern.
Resend email sending domain must be DNS-verified (DKIM + SPF) before production use. Unverified domains will land in spam or be rejected entirely.
Airtable free plan has 1,200 records per base and 5 API calls per second. High-volume Shopify stores will hit both limits quickly — plan for Airtable Pro or an alternative inventory backend.

goal: Streamline design-to-development handoff
description: When a Figma design is finalized, create a GitHub issue with specs, track it in Linear, and notify the dev team in Discord.

steps.action: Detect file version published event and extract frame details and export URLs
steps.error_hint: Configure Figma webhook for FILE_VERSION_UPDATE event type. File key is in the Figma URL.

steps.action: Create an implementation issue with Figma link and design specs
steps.error_hint: Create the 'design-handoff' label in the GitHub repo beforehand

steps.action: Create linked task for implementation tracking with GitHub issue reference
steps.error_hint: Use Linear's GitHub integration to auto-link issues if available

steps.action: Post design handoff notification with links to the dev channel
steps.error_hint: Use Discord embeds for richer formatting with preview images

gotchas: Figma webhooks only fire for the FILE_VERSION_UPDATE event when a file version is explicitly published (not on every auto-save) — instruct designers to use File > Save to Version History to trigger the workflow.

GitHub label creation is a separate API call from issue creation — if 'design-handoff' label does not exist in the repo, adding it to the issue will fail silently (the issue is created without the label, no error returned).

Linear team_id and label resolution require separate GraphQL queries — the API does not accept human-readable team names or label names directly. Cache these UUIDs in config rather than querying on every webhook.
Discord embed URLs must be HTTPS — Figma file URLs (https://figma.com/file/...) work, but if you construct URLs with http:// by mistake, Discord will not render the embed preview.

goal: AI-powered automated code review on pull requests
description: Fetch a GitHub PR diff, analyze it with Anthropic API for quality and security issues, and post a review comment.

steps.action: Receive PR event and fetch the full diff content
steps.error_hint: Use the application/vnd.github.v3.diff media type to get raw diff. Large diffs may need truncation.

steps.action: Analyze diff for bugs, security issues, performance problems, and style violations

input_mapping.prompt:
Review this code diff. Identify bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and suggest improvements:
{{step1.diff_content}}

steps.error_hint: Truncate diff to last 8000 tokens if too large. Focus on changed lines only.

steps.action: Post AI review as a PR comment with structured feedback

input_mapping.body:
## AI Code Review
{{step2.review}}

---
_Automated review by KanseiLink_

steps.error_hint: Use GitHub review comments API for inline suggestions. Comment body max is 65536 characters.

gotchas: GitHub PR diffs can exceed 100KB for large refactors — the Anthropic API will reject inputs over the model's context window. Truncate the diff to changed hunks only (skip lockfiles, generated code) and cap at ~8000 tokens.

Posting a review comment via the GitHub Issues Comments API has a 65,536-character body limit — if the Anthropic response is very long, split it into multiple comments or use the Pull Request Reviews API for inline comments.
The Anthropic API requires an explicit max_tokens parameter — omitting it returns an error, unlike OpenAI which defaults to model max. Always set max_tokens in the request.

goal: Generate and distribute automated data reports
description: Pull analytics data from Supabase, generate an AI summary report, post to Slack, and archive in Notion.

steps.action: Query analytics tables for key metrics from the past period

input_mapping.query: SELECT metric, value, period FROM analytics WHERE period = current_week()

steps.error_hint: Use Supabase RPC functions for complex queries. Ensure RLS policies allow service role access.

steps.action: Generate executive summary and trend analysis from raw metrics

input_mapping.prompt:
Generate a concise weekly report from these metrics. Highlight trends, anomalies, and recommendations:
{{step1.data}}

steps.error_hint: Structure the prompt to request specific sections: Summary, Trends, Action Items

steps.action: Post the weekly report to the leadership channel
steps.error_hint: Pin the message automatically for easy reference throughout the week

steps.action: Archive report as a dated page in the reports database
steps.error_hint: Use Notion's date property type for the Period field to enable calendar views

gotchas: Supabase RPC/SQL queries respect Row Level Security by default — use the service_role key (not anon key) for analytics queries, or the query will return zero rows if no user context is set.

OpenAI rate limits vary by tier — on Tier 1 accounts, gpt-4o is limited to ~500 RPM and 30K TPM. If the analytics payload is large, you may hit token-per-minute limits. Summarize raw data before sending to the LLM.
Notion API blocks have a 2000-block limit per request and rich text blocks max at 2000 characters each — a long AI-generated report must be chunked into multiple append calls.
Slack messages over 4000 characters are truncated in the preview — use Block Kit with multiple section blocks, or post a summary and thread the full report as a follow-up.

goal: Full-stack new user signup automation
description: After Stripe checkout, create Firebase auth user, add to Mailchimp audience, and send a Discord welcome.

steps.action: Receive successful checkout session with customer details and plan info
steps.error_hint: Handle duplicate webhook deliveries idempotently using the checkout session ID

steps.action: Create authenticated user with email and custom claims for plan access
steps.error_hint: Check if user already exists before creation. Use Firebase Admin SDK for custom claims.

steps.action: Add subscriber to the product mailing list with plan-based tags
steps.error_hint: Use Mailchimp's PUT endpoint to handle existing subscribers (upsert behavior)

steps.action: Send welcome DM via bot with getting-started guide link

input_mapping.message: Welcome to {{config.app_name}}, {{step1.customer_name}}! Here's how to get started: {{config.onboarding_url}}

steps.error_hint: User must be in the Discord server first. Fall back to welcome channel mention if DM fails.

gotchas: Discord DMs require the bot to share a server with the user AND the user must have DMs enabled — matching by email is not a native Discord operation. You need a prior mapping of email-to-Discord-user-ID, or fall back to posting a welcome in a public channel.
Firebase createUser will throw auth/email-already-exists if the Stripe webhook retries — wrap the call in a try-catch and treat 'already exists' as a success, then update custom claims.

Mailchimp's PUT member endpoint (upsert) requires the subscriber hash (MD5 of lowercase email) in the URL path — using the raw email in the path returns a 404. Always lowercase and MD5-hash the email.

Stripe checkout.session.completed webhook signatures use the raw request body — if your middleware parses JSON before verification, the signature check will fail. Use the raw body buffer for verification.

goal: Monitor deployments with automated health checks
description: After a Vercel deployment completes, run a health check, update Grafana, and alert Slack if issues are detected.

steps.action: Receive deployment completed webhook with URL and deployment metadata
steps.error_hint: Filter for deployment.ready events only — ignore deployment.created to avoid premature checks

steps.action: Run health check against the deployment URL and verify response status
steps.error_hint: Add retry logic with 30s delay — new deployments may need time to warm up

steps.action: Push deployment event and health metrics to Grafana dashboard
steps.error_hint: Use Grafana's push gateway for custom metrics. Ensure data source is configured.

steps.action: Post deployment result — green for healthy, red alert if health check failed
steps.error_hint: Use different channels for healthy vs unhealthy deploys to manage alert noise

gotchas: Vercel fires deployment.created, deployment.ready, and deployment.error events — subscribe only to deployment.ready and deployment.error, or your health check will hit a URL that is still building and return a false-negative 502.

New Vercel deployments use unique preview URLs (e.g., project-abc123.vercel.app) that differ from the production domain — health-check the actual deployment URL, not the production alias, to test the specific deploy.
Grafana Cloud's annotation API requires the dashboard numeric ID (not the UID slug) — use the dashboard search API to resolve the slug to an ID first, or annotations will 404.

goal: Automate meeting preparation and follow-up
description: When a Calendly meeting is booked, create a Notion prep page, send a prep email via Resend, and set a Slack reminder.

steps.action: Receive invitee.created webhook with meeting details and attendee info
steps.error_hint: Subscribe to invitee.created event type. Calendly webhooks require HTTPS endpoints.

steps.action: Create a meeting prep page with attendee info, agenda template, and notes section
steps.error_hint: Use ISO 8601 date format for the Date property

steps.action: Send internal prep email with meeting details and Notion page link
steps.error_hint: Create the meeting-prep email template in Resend dashboard first

steps.action: Post meeting reminder to the team channel with prep page link
steps.error_hint: Use Slack's scheduled messages API (chat.scheduleMessage) for the 30-min reminder

gotchas: Calendly webhook payloads nest attendee data under invitee.questions_and_answers as an array of objects — each Q&A has 'question' and 'answer' fields, not a flat key-value map. Parse accordingly.

Slack's chat.scheduleMessage API requires the post_at timestamp in Unix epoch seconds and the time must be between 1-120 days in the future — scheduling a reminder 30 minutes before a meeting happening in 15 minutes will fail with 'time_in_past'.
Resend email templates are referenced by template ID (a UUID), not by name — 'meeting-prep' is not a valid template reference. Look up the template ID from the Resend dashboard or Templates API.

Notion date properties require ISO 8601 format with timezone — Calendly returns times in UTC. If your Notion database expects a local timezone, convert before writing or the meeting time will display incorrectly.

goal: AI-predicted infrastructure scaling alerts
description: Analyze Cloudflare traffic analytics with AI to predict scaling needs and proactively alert the team.

steps.action: Fetch traffic analytics, request rates, and bandwidth data for the past 24 hours
steps.error_hint: Use Cloudflare GraphQL Analytics API for more flexible queries. API token needs Analytics:Read permission.

steps.action: Analyze traffic patterns and predict if scaling action is needed

input_mapping.prompt:
Analyze this web traffic data and predict if the infrastructure needs scaling in the next 6 hours. Provide confidence level and recommended action:
{{step1.analytics}}

steps.error_hint: Include historical baseline data in the prompt for more accurate predictions

steps.action: Create a warning event if AI predicts scaling is needed

input_mapping.summary: Predicted traffic spike — scaling may be needed

steps.error_hint: Use PagerDuty Events API v2. Set dedup_key to prevent duplicate alerts for the same prediction.

steps.action: Post traffic analysis summary to the infrastructure channel
steps.error_hint: Include a link to the Cloudflare dashboard for the team to verify predictions

gotchas: Cloudflare Analytics API (GraphQL) requires an API token with the Analytics:Read permission at the zone level — the Global API Key works but is a security risk. Use a scoped token.

PagerDuty Events API v2 requires a routing_key tied to a specific service — the 'service_id' in config must be the integration key from the service's Events API v2 integration, not the service's PagerDuty ID.

OpenAI token limits apply to both input and output — large Cloudflare analytics payloads (24 hours of per-minute data) can exceed context limits. Aggregate data to hourly buckets before sending to the LLM.
PagerDuty dedup_key should be deterministic for the same prediction window — use a hash of date + zone_id to prevent duplicate alerts. Without dedup, each cron run creates a new incident.

goal: AI-powered open source issue triage and classification
description: When a GitHub issue is created, use AI to classify it, auto-assign labels, and notify maintainers in Discord.

steps.action: Receive issue opened event with title, body, and author info
steps.error_hint: Filter webhook for issues action=opened only to avoid updates and closures

steps.action: Classify issue type (bug, feature, question, docs) and estimate priority
steps.error_hint: Use structured JSON output format. Add few-shot examples in the system prompt for consistency.

steps.action: Apply AI-suggested labels and add a classification comment

input_mapping.comment:
Auto-classified as **{{step2.type}}** (priority: {{step2.priority}})

_Classified by KanseiLink AI triage_

steps.error_hint: Ensure all labels in the classification exist in the repo. Create missing labels via API first.

steps.action: Notify maintainers channel with classified issue summary
steps.error_hint: Use Discord role mentions for high-priority issues to alert the right maintainers

gotchas: Anthropic API JSON mode is not guaranteed to return valid JSON — always wrap the response parse in try-catch. Use a system prompt with explicit JSON schema and few-shot examples to improve reliability.

GitHub label application requires the labels to already exist in the repository — if the AI suggests a label like 'performance' that does not exist, the API returns 422. Pre-create expected labels or create them via API before applying.

GitHub API rate limit is 5000 requests/hour with a token, but popular open-source repos can receive hundreds of issues per day — batch webhook processing and use conditional requests (If-None-Match) to conserve quota.
Discord role mentions (@maintainers) require the bot to have the Mention Everyone permission or the role to be set as mentionable — otherwise the mention renders as plain text with no notification.

goal: Automate invoice processing and notifications
description: When a Stripe invoice is finalized, log it to Airtable, notify the finance team in Slack, and email the receipt.

steps.action: Receive invoice.finalized webhook with line items and customer info
steps.error_hint: Listen for invoice.finalized not invoice.created — finalized means the invoice is ready to send

steps.action: Create invoice record in the finance tracking table
steps.error_hint: Use Airtable field validation to ensure amount is a positive number

steps.action: Notify finance channel with invoice summary
steps.error_hint: Format currency amounts consistently — use Intl.NumberFormat or similar

steps.action: Email the invoice receipt to the customer
steps.error_hint: Stripe can also send invoices natively — disable Stripe emails if using this workflow to avoid duplicates

gotchas: Stripe amounts are in the smallest currency unit (cents for USD, yen for JPY) — Airtable and Resend templates expect human-readable amounts. Divide by 100 for cent-based currencies, but NOT for zero-decimal currencies like JPY.

Stripe can send its own invoice emails by default — if you do not disable Stripe's automatic emails in Dashboard > Settings > Emails, customers will receive duplicate invoices (one from Stripe, one from Resend).
Airtable API rate limit is 5 requests per second per base — if multiple invoices finalize in a burst (e.g., subscription renewal day), queue writes or use batch create (up to 10 records per call).
Resend email templates must be created in the dashboard before the API can reference them — a missing template_id returns a 422 error that does not clearly indicate the template is missing.

goal: Automated security vulnerability response pipeline
description: When GitHub Dependabot finds a vulnerability, create a Linear issue, notify Slack, and log it in Notion.

steps.action: Receive Dependabot alert webhook with vulnerability details and severity
steps.error_hint: Enable Dependabot alerts in GitHub repo settings. Webhook event type is 'dependabot_alert'.

steps.action: Create security fix issue with vulnerability details and priority based on severity

input_mapping.title: Security: {{step1.package_name}} vulnerability ({{step1.severity}})
input_mapping.label: security

steps.error_hint: Map Dependabot severity (critical/high/medium/low) to Linear priority (1-4)

steps.action: Alert the security channel with vulnerability details and remediation link
steps.error_hint: Use @here mention for critical severity alerts

steps.action: Log vulnerability in the security audit database for compliance tracking
steps.error_hint: Use Notion select property for Severity to enable filtering and views

gotchas: Dependabot severity levels (critical, high, medium, low) do not directly map to Linear priority numbers (1=urgent, 2=high, 3=medium, 4=low) — build an explicit mapping or critical alerts will be created with the wrong priority.

GitHub Dependabot webhooks require the repository to have Dependabot alerts enabled AND the webhook must subscribe to the 'dependabot_alert' event type — this is a separate event from 'security_advisory'.

Linear's GraphQL API uses UUIDs for team_id and label IDs, not human-readable names — resolve 'security' label name to its UUID via the labels query before creating the issue, or the mutation will fail.

Notion select properties must use pre-defined options — if you pass a Severity value that does not exist as a select option in the database, the API returns a validation error. Pre-create all four severity options.

goal: Coordinate multi-service deployment across platforms
description: On GitHub push, deploy frontend to Vercel and backend to Netlify, then verify health and notify Discord.

steps.action: Detect push to main branch and determine which directories changed
steps.error_hint: Use path filters to detect if frontend/, backend/, or both directories changed

steps.action: Deploy frontend application and wait for ready status
steps.error_hint: Set a deployment timeout of 10 minutes. Check build logs if deployment gets stuck.

steps.action: Deploy backend service and wait for published status
steps.error_hint: Netlify Functions have a 10s execution timeout by default. Check function logs for errors.

steps.action: Post deployment summary with both service statuses and URLs
steps.error_hint: Use color-coded embeds: green for both healthy, yellow if one failed, red if both failed

gotchas: Vercel and Netlify deploy hooks both return immediately and require polling for completion — if you notify Discord before both are done, the status will be 'building' not 'ready'. Poll both with independent timeouts (Vercel ~10min, Netlify ~5min).

Vercel environment variables are scoped per-project and per-environment (production/preview/development) — if the frontend deploy fails with missing env vars, check that production-scoped variables are set, not just preview.
Discord message embeds have a 6000-character total limit across all fields — if both deploy logs are included, truncate build output to essential lines only.

GitHub push webhooks include a 'modified_files' list only for the head commit — for force-pushes or squash merges, the list may be incomplete. Use the Compare API to get the full diff between before/after SHAs.

goal: AI-assisted customer support with human review
description: When a support message arrives in Discord, draft an AI response with Anthropic, create a review task in Linear, then reply.

steps.action: Receive message in the support channel and extract question details
steps.error_hint: Filter for messages in the support channel only. Ignore bot messages to prevent loops.

steps.action: Generate a helpful response draft based on documentation and FAQ context

input_mapping.system: You are a helpful support agent. Use the product documentation to answer questions accurately. If unsure, say so.

steps.error_hint: Include relevant FAQ/docs context in the system prompt. Keep responses concise and actionable.

steps.action: Create a support review task with the AI draft for human approval

input_mapping.title: Support review: {{step1.author_name}}
input_mapping.label: support

steps.error_hint: Set up Linear automation to notify the on-call support person when this task is created

gotchas: Discord bot must explicitly filter out its own messages and other bot messages to prevent infinite response loops. Check the message author's bot flag before processing.
Anthropic API responses may vary in length and format. Set max_tokens appropriately and implement a truncation strategy for Discord's 2000-character message limit.

This recipe creates a review task in Linear but does not auto-reply to the customer. The human-in-the-loop step is intentional — make sure the support team knows to check Linear and manually post the approved response.
Discord webhook/bot gateway connections can drop silently. Implement heartbeat monitoring and automatic reconnection to avoid missing support messages.

goal: Development workflow: PR to task tracking with team notifications
description: Automate development lifecycle: GitHub PR triggers Linear issue update, Slack notifies the team of progress and review requests.

steps.action: Monitor pull request events (opened, merged, review requested)
steps.error_hint: Ensure GitHub webhook is configured for PR events

steps.action: Update linked issue status based on PR state (In Review, Done)
steps.error_hint: Branch name must contain Linear issue ID (e.g., ENG-123)

steps.action: Post PR status update to engineering channel
steps.error_hint: Bot must be invited to the target channel

gotchas: Linear issue linking depends on the branch name containing the issue ID (e.g., 'ENG-123-fix-bug') — if developers use non-standard branch names, the PR-to-issue lookup fails. Document the naming convention or parse from PR body.
Linear's GraphQL API uses cursor-based pagination (Relay style) — if you query issues by identifier and get no result, check that you are using the correct team prefix (e.g., ENG, not ENGINEERING).

GitHub pull_request webhooks fire for multiple actions (opened, synchronize, review_requested, closed) — map each action to the correct Linear status transition, or status will only update on open, not on merge.

goal: Payment to accounting sync with notifications
description: Stripe payment events automatically create QuickBooks journal entries and notify finance team via Slack.

steps.action: Capture payment_intent.succeeded webhook with amount, currency, customer
steps.error_hint: Ensure Stripe webhook endpoint is configured for payment_intent.succeeded

steps.action: Create sales receipt or invoice payment in QuickBooks
steps.error_hint: Customer must exist in QuickBooks. Map Stripe customer ID to QBO customer

steps.action: Notify finance channel of recorded payment
steps.error_hint: Verify bot has access to finance channel

gotchas: Stripe customer IDs do not exist in QuickBooks. You must maintain a mapping between Stripe customer_id and QuickBooks customer DisplayName, or the journal entry will fail to associate correctly.

Stripe amounts are in the smallest currency unit (cents for USD, but JPY is zero-decimal). Verify currency handling — passing raw JPY amounts to QuickBooks as if they were cents will create 100x overcharges.
QuickBooks OAuth2 tokens expire in 1 hour, and refresh tokens expire in 100 days. Implement proactive token refresh or the integration will break silently after token expiry.
Stripe webhook signature verification (using endpoint secret) is critical for production. Without it, anyone can POST fake payment events to your endpoint and create bogus QuickBooks entries.

goal: Ecommerce analytics and marketing automation pipeline
description: Shopify order events flow through Segment CDP to Klaviyo for targeted email campaigns and customer segmentation.

steps.action: Capture order events (created, fulfilled, refunded) via webhook
steps.error_hint: Configure Shopify webhook for orders/create and orders/fulfilled

steps.action: Track order event with customer properties to Segment CDP
steps.error_hint: Ensure Segment source is configured for server-side tracking

steps.action: Trigger post-purchase email flow based on order data
steps.error_hint: Klaviyo flow must be active for Placed Order metric

gotchas: Segment event names must match Klaviyo's expected metric names exactly. Klaviyo uses 'Placed Order' and 'Ordered Product' — if Segment sends 'Order Completed', the Klaviyo flow will not trigger.
Shopify Global (non-JP) webhooks use USD amounts by default. If your store uses multiple currencies, ensure Segment passes the correct currency code or Klaviyo revenue tracking will be inaccurate.
Segment to Klaviyo destination must be configured as a server-side integration, not client-side. Client-side tracking misses webhook-originated events entirely.
Klaviyo flows must be set to 'Live' status — draft or paused flows will silently ignore incoming events. Verify flow status after initial setup and after any edits.

goal: Sales meeting booking and follow-up automation
description: Calendly meeting booked creates HubSpot deal, Gmail sends pre-meeting prep email with prospect context.

steps.action: Capture meeting scheduled event with attendee details
steps.error_hint: Calendly webhook must be subscribed to invitee.created events

steps.action: Create or update deal in HubSpot with meeting details
steps.error_hint: Check if contact already exists in HubSpot before creating

steps.action: Send pre-meeting prep email to sales rep with prospect context
steps.error_hint: Ensure Gmail API send scope is authorized

gotchas: Calendly webhook payload includes invitee email — use this to look up the HubSpot contact, not the event name
Google Workspace (Gmail) API requires OAuth consent with mail.send scope — this is a sensitive scope requiring Google verification
Calendly event types have UUIDs — store the mapping between event type names and UUIDs in configuration
HubSpot meeting engagement logging requires the contact vid and timestamp — Calendly provides ISO 8601 timestamps, HubSpot expects Unix milliseconds

goal: Design handoff to production deployment pipeline
description: Figma design updates trigger GitHub issue for implementation, Vercel deploys preview for design review.

steps.action: Detect design file version update and extract component changes
steps.error_hint: Figma webhook must be registered via Figma REST API

steps.action: Create GitHub issue with design change details and Figma link
steps.error_hint: GitHub token needs repo:write permission

steps.action: Trigger preview deployment for the design branch
steps.error_hint: Vercel project must be connected to the GitHub repo

gotchas: Figma webhooks must be registered via the Figma REST API (POST /v2/webhooks) — there is no UI for this. The webhook secret is returned only once at creation time; store it immediately for signature verification.

Vercel preview deployments require a branch ref, not a commit SHA — if the GitHub issue creation step does not also create a branch, the Vercel deploy step has no ref to build. Automate branch creation from the issue.
Figma file_key is extracted from the URL (figma.com/file/{file_key}/...) and is case-sensitive — passing a malformed key returns a 404 from the Figma API with no helpful error message.
Vercel preview URLs are ephemeral and change with every deploy — do not store them as permanent links. Use the deployment's inspect URL or alias for stable references.

goal: B2B contract flow: deal close to signature to invoicing
description: Salesforce deal closure triggers DocuSign contract, signed contract auto-creates QuickBooks invoice.

steps.action: Detect opportunity stage change to Closed Won
steps.error_hint: Configure Salesforce outbound message for Opportunity stage change

steps.action: Send contract envelope for e-signature to customer
steps.error_hint: DocuSign template must have merge fields matching Salesforce data

steps.action: Create invoice upon contract signature completion
steps.error_hint: Customer name must match between Salesforce and QuickBooks

gotchas: DocuSign envelope template merge fields must match Salesforce field API names exactly. A mismatch means the contract will be sent with blank fields — always test with a sandbox Salesforce org first.
DocuSign demo vs production environments use completely different base URLs (demo.docusign.net vs docusign.net). Envelopes created in demo do not exist in production — switch URLs when going live.
QuickBooks customer names must match between Salesforce account names and QuickBooks. Japanese company suffixes (株式会社, 合同会社) and whitespace differences cause silent matching failures.

The contract signing step is asynchronous — the customer may take days to sign. Your integration must handle the DocuSign Connect callback (webhook) to trigger QuickBooks invoice creation only after completion.

goal: Customer support escalation to engineering bug tracking
description: Zendesk ticket tagged as bug automatically creates Linear issue and notifies engineering via Slack.

steps.action: Detect ticket tagged with 'bug' or priority escalation
steps.error_hint: Zendesk trigger must fire on tag addition or priority change

steps.action: Create bug issue in engineering team with ticket context
steps.error_hint: Linear API key must have issue create permission

steps.action: Alert engineering channel with bug details and Linear link
steps.error_hint: Bot must be in engineering channel

gotchas: Linear API is GraphQL-only — use mutations for issue creation, not REST endpoints
Linear team IDs and label IDs are UUIDs — cannot use human-readable names directly in API calls
Zendesk ticket HTML content needs conversion to Linear markdown — strip HTML tags and convert basic formatting
Slack thread_ts is required for posting the Linear issue link as a reply — without it, the link goes to the channel root

goal: Document sharing and knowledge base sync
description: New Google Drive files in shared folder are indexed in Notion wiki and team is notified via Slack.

steps.action: Watch shared folder for new file uploads
steps.error_hint: Google Drive push notification channel must be set up

steps.action: Add entry to knowledge base database with file link and metadata
steps.error_hint: Notion integration must have access to the target database

steps.action: Notify team channel about new document

input_mapping.message: New doc: {{file_name}} by {{created_by}} — added to wiki

steps.error_hint: Verify Slack bot token has chat:write scope

gotchas: Google Drive push notification channels expire (default 1 hour, max 24 hours for most resources). You must implement automatic channel renewal or your file-watching integration will stop working silently.

Notion API integration must be explicitly shared with the target database in Notion's 'Connections' settings. Without this, API calls will return 'object not found' errors even with a valid database ID.
Google Drive webhook sends only the file ID and change type — not file metadata. You need a follow-up files.get API call to retrieve the file name, owner, and web link for the Notion entry.
Slack bot token must have chat:write scope AND the bot must be invited to the target channel. A common failure: the bot has permission but was never added to a private channel.

goal: Startup financial operations: payments, banking, and expense tracking
description: Stripe revenue flows to Mercury banking, Brex expense data synced for unified financial visibility.

steps.action: Aggregate daily payment summary (revenue, fees, net)
steps.error_hint: Use Stripe Balance Transactions API with date filter

steps.action: Verify Stripe payout received in Mercury checking account
steps.error_hint: Mercury API may have delay in reflecting pending transactions

steps.action: Pull daily card spend and categorize against budget
steps.error_hint: Brex API pagination may be needed for high transaction volume

gotchas: Stripe payouts to Mercury have a 2-3 business day delay. When reconciling, do not expect same-day matching — use the Stripe payout.paid event (not payment_intent.succeeded) to trigger Mercury verification.
Mercury API may not reflect pending transactions immediately. Add a retry mechanism with exponential backoff when checking for Stripe payout arrival.
Brex API uses pagination for transaction listings. On days with many card transactions, a single API call will not return all data — implement cursor-based pagination.

Currency handling: Stripe reports in the settlement currency, Mercury in USD, and Brex in the transaction currency. Normalize all amounts to a single currency before creating unified financial reports.

goal: Unified product analytics pipeline
description: Segment collects events and routes to both Mixpanel and Amplitude for cross-platform product analysis.

steps.action: Collect and normalize user events from web and mobile
steps.error_hint: Segment source must be configured for each platform (web, iOS, Android)

steps.action: Route event to Mixpanel for funnel and retention analysis
steps.error_hint: Segment destination for Mixpanel must be enabled and mapped

steps.action: Route event to Amplitude for behavioral cohort analysis
steps.error_hint: Segment destination for Amplitude must be enabled

gotchas: Segment event property names must be consistent across all sources (web, iOS, Android). A property named 'user_id' on web and 'userId' on mobile will create duplicate properties in both Mixpanel and Amplitude.
Segment destination configurations for Mixpanel and Amplitude must each be enabled and properly mapped. A disabled destination will silently drop events with no error in Segment.

Mixpanel and Amplitude have different identity resolution models. Mixpanel uses distinct_id while Amplitude uses user_id + device_id. If Segment's identity mapping is misconfigured, you will get inflated user counts in one or both tools.

Segment server-side tracking does not capture client-side context (device info, IP, user agent) automatically. Add these properties explicitly if they are needed for Mixpanel/Amplitude behavioral analysis.

goal: AI-powered research automation with knowledge capture
description: Brave Search retrieves web data, Anthropic Claude analyzes and summarizes, results stored in Notion research database.

steps.action: Search web for topic and retrieve top results with snippets
steps.error_hint: Brave Search API key required. Rate limit: 1 req/sec on free tier

steps.action: Analyze search results and generate structured research summary

input_mapping.prompt: Analyze these search results about {{research_topic}} and provide key findings

steps.error_hint: Keep token usage within API limits

steps.action: Create research entry in knowledge base with summary and sources
steps.error_hint: Notion page content blocks have 2000 character limit per block

gotchas: Brave Search API free tier is limited to 1 request per second and 2,000 queries per month. Automated research workflows can exhaust this quota quickly — upgrade to a paid plan for production use.
Anthropic API token costs scale with input size. Passing all 10 search result snippets as context can be expensive. Summarize or truncate search results before sending to Claude to control costs.
Notion API has a 3 requests per second rate limit. If creating research pages in rapid succession (e.g., batch research), add delays between API calls to avoid 429 errors.

Brave Search results may include unreliable sources. The Anthropic analysis step should include source credibility evaluation in its prompt to avoid propagating misinformation into your Notion knowledge base.

goal: Feature flag monitoring with automated incident response
description: PostHog detects error spike after feature flag rollout, alerts Slack, auto-creates Linear incident ticket.

steps.action: Monitor error rate for feature flag cohort and detect anomaly
steps.error_hint: PostHog action webhook must be configured for error rate threshold

steps.action: Send urgent alert to engineering channel with rollback recommendation

input_mapping.message: Feature flag {{feature_flag}} error spike: {{error_rate}}% vs {{baseline_rate}}% baseline. {{affected_users}} users affected.

steps.error_hint: Use Slack blocks for rich formatting of incident data

steps.action: Create urgent incident issue with feature flag context
steps.error_hint: Ensure 'incident' label exists in Linear team

gotchas: PostHog action webhooks send a generic payload — error_rate and baseline_rate are not included by default. You must configure a PostHog Action with a webhook that includes custom properties from your event schema.

Linear priority 1 (Urgent) triggers immediate notifications to assignees — if no assignee is set, the urgent issue goes unnoticed. Always set a default assignee or use a team-level auto-assignment rule.

PostHog feature flag evaluation is eventually consistent — there can be a delay between flag rollout and error data appearing. Set a minimum observation window (e.g., 15 minutes) before triggering the alert to avoid false positives.

Slack messages with @channel or @here in the text field require the bot to have chat:write permission and the channel to allow bot mentions — otherwise the mention is silently stripped from the posted message.

goal: Weekly PostgreSQL schema audit with AI-generated summary
description: Agent connects to production Postgres via the official MCP server, inspects schemas and table sizes, asks an LLM to summarize changes since last week, and posts a digest to Slack.

steps.action: List all schemas, tables, row counts, and index sizes (read-only)
steps.error_hint: Anthropic's postgres-mcp is read-only by design. Ensure the DB user has SELECT on information_schema and pg_catalog.

steps.action: Ask GPT to diff this week's snapshot against last week's and write a plain-English summary

input_mapping.system: You are a database change reviewer. Flag new tables, dropped columns, and row-count swings >20%.

steps.error_hint: Truncate large snapshots to stay under the model's context window. Use a structured JSON diff first if tables >200.

steps.action: Post the weekly schema digest to the database-ops channel
steps.error_hint: Use Block Kit sections for long summaries — plain text over ~3000 chars may be truncated in the preview.

gotchas: The postgresql-mcp server runs queries with the credentials of the configured database user — ensure this user has SELECT on information_schema.tables, information_schema.columns, and pg_catalog.pg_stat_user_tables, or the audit will return empty results.

Comparing this week's snapshot to last week's requires persisting the previous snapshot somewhere (config store, file, or database) — Slack messages are not reliable storage. Store snapshots in a dedicated table or object store.

Large databases with 200+ tables can produce schema snapshots exceeding the OpenAI context window (128K tokens for gpt-4o) — pre-filter to changed tables only or use a structured JSON diff before sending to the LLM.

pg_stat_statements and pg_stat_user_tables row counts are estimates from the planner — for exact counts, the query needs COUNT(*) per table, which can be slow on large tables. Use the estimate for audits and note the caveat.

goal: Build a RAG knowledge base from GitHub docs into Qdrant
description: Agent pulls markdown docs from a GitHub repo, generates embeddings via OpenAI, upserts them into a Qdrant collection with metadata, and reports the indexing stats to Slack.

steps.action: List and fetch all .md files under the /docs path of the source repo
steps.error_hint: Files >1MB require the Git Data API (blobs), not /contents. Use fine-grained PAT with contents:read scope.

steps.action: Generate embeddings for each chunked document (text-embedding-3-small)
steps.error_hint: Batch embedding requests (up to 2048 inputs per call) to avoid rate limits. Strip code blocks if not needed for search.

steps.action: Upsert vectors with document metadata into the docs collection
steps.error_hint: Create the collection first with matching vector size (1536 for text-embedding-3-small). Use recreate=true only for full reindex.

steps.action: Post indexing summary (doc count, vector count, duration) to the AI-ops channel
steps.error_hint: Include the commit SHA so the team can reproduce the index state.

gotchas: The Qdrant collection must be created with the correct vector dimension BEFORE upserting — text-embedding-3-small produces 1536-dimensional vectors. A dimension mismatch returns an error that does not clearly state the cause.

GitHub's /contents API returns Base64-encoded file content and has a 1MB file size limit — for larger docs, use the Git Blobs API. Also, the API paginates directory listings at 1000 files; use the Git Trees API for larger doc sets.
OpenAI embedding API accepts up to 2048 inputs per batch call — but total tokens across all inputs must stay under 8191 tokens per input. Chunk documents before batching, not after.

Qdrant distance metric (Cosine, Euclid, Dot) is set at collection creation and cannot be changed — OpenAI embeddings are normalized, so Cosine and Dot produce identical rankings, but Euclid does not. Use Cosine for OpenAI embeddings.

goal: Answer support questions from a Qdrant-backed knowledge base
description: When a user posts a question in Slack, the agent embeds the query with OpenAI, finds the top-5 most similar passages in Qdrant, generates an answer grounded in those passages, and replies in-thread.

steps.action: Receive @mention in a support channel and extract the question text
steps.error_hint: Strip the @bot mention from the text before embedding. Use event_ts as thread_ts if thread_ts is missing.

steps.action: Embed the question with the same model used for indexing
steps.error_hint: Use the SAME embedding model as the index build — mismatched models produce meaningless similarity scores.

steps.action: Similarity search: top-5 nearest passages with payload metadata
steps.error_hint: A score_threshold filters out irrelevant results. Tune it based on your corpus — typically 0.6-0.8 for cosine similarity.

steps.action: Generate an answer grounded ONLY in the retrieved passages

input_mapping.system: Answer the user's question using ONLY the provided passages. If the answer isn't there, say 'I don't know'.

steps.error_hint: Explicitly instruct the model to refuse when the passages don't contain the answer — this prevents hallucination.

steps.action: Reply in-thread with the answer and citations
steps.error_hint: Always cite source paths so users can verify. In-thread replies prevent channel noise.

gotchas: The embedding model for queries MUST match the model used during indexing — if the knowledge base was built with text-embedding-3-small but queries use text-embedding-ada-002, similarity scores will be meaningless and results random.

Slack app_mention events require the Event Subscriptions feature enabled with the app_mentions:read scope — this is separate from bot token scopes. Missing this scope means the bot never receives mention events.

Qdrant score_threshold varies by distance metric — for Cosine similarity, 0.7 is a reasonable threshold, but for Dot product on unnormalized vectors, scores can exceed 1.0. Calibrate the threshold to your specific corpus.

Slack messages have a 4000-character limit for the text field — if the RAG answer plus citations exceeds this, the message silently truncates. Use Block Kit blocks or split into a summary message and threaded detail.

goal: Nightly sync of MongoDB Atlas collections into a Neon Postgres analytics warehouse
description: Agent reads new/changed documents from MongoDB Atlas via the Data API, transforms them into relational rows, writes them to Neon Postgres for analytics, and reports row counts to Slack.

steps.action: Query the source collection for documents updated since the last sync checkpoint
steps.error_hint: Use projection to reduce payload size. Paginate with skip/limit if the collection is large — the Data API limits response size.

steps.action: Upsert rows into the analytics.mongo_sync table (ON CONFLICT updated_at)

input_mapping.sql: INSERT INTO mongo_sync (id, data, updated_at) VALUES ... ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET data = EXCLUDED.data, updated_at = EXCLUDED.updated_at

steps.error_hint: Neon supports branching — consider syncing into a branch first, then promoting. JSONB is the right type for schemaless Mongo docs.

steps.action: Post sync summary and the new checkpoint for operators to verify
steps.error_hint: Also persist the new_checkpoint to config storage — Slack is for humans, not agent state.

gotchas: MongoDB Atlas Data API has a 16MB response limit and a default timeout of 10 seconds — if the changed document set is large, paginate with skip/limit and add projection to fetch only needed fields.

Neon Postgres serverless connections auto-suspend after 5 minutes of inactivity — the first query after suspension has a ~1s cold start. For nightly syncs this is fine, but set a connection timeout > 5s to handle the wake-up.
MongoDB ObjectId fields do not directly map to Postgres types — store them as TEXT or UUID in the target table. Casting errors will cause the entire INSERT batch to fail with no partial commit.

The sync checkpoint (last_sync_ts) must be persisted outside of Slack — if the agent relies on parsing the last Slack message to find the checkpoint, message edits or deletions will break the sync. Store it in a config table or environment variable.

goal: Warm an Upstash Redis cache with hot data from PostgreSQL before peak hours
description: Before a daily traffic peak, the agent reads the most-accessed entities from PostgreSQL via the official MCP server, writes them into Upstash Redis with a TTL, and reports cache fill stats to Teams.

steps.action: Read the top-N hot rows from the source table (e.g., products by view count last 7d)

input_mapping.query: SELECT id, data FROM products ORDER BY views_7d DESC LIMIT 500

steps.error_hint: postgres-mcp is read-only by design. Aggregate views_7d via a materialized view if the OLTP table is hot.

steps.action: SET each row as a JSON value with a 6-hour TTL
steps.error_hint: Use the Upstash REST API pipeline endpoint for batch writes — single SETs will hit rate limits on large warmups.

steps.action: Post cache warmup completion to the platform-ops Teams channel
steps.error_hint: Teams Graph API requires Chat.ReadWrite or ChannelMessage.Send. Use Adaptive Cards for richer formatting.

gotchas: Upstash Redis REST API has a rate limit of ~1000 commands/second on the free tier — warming 500 keys with individual SET calls will take 500 requests. Use the pipeline endpoint (POST /pipeline) to batch all SETs in a single HTTP call.

The postgresql-mcp server is strictly read-only — do not attempt to update a 'last_warmed_at' timestamp through it. Use a separate write path or track cache warm status in Redis itself with a metadata key.

Microsoft Teams Graph API requires delegated or application permissions (ChannelMessage.Send) and an Azure AD app registration — this is significantly more setup than Slack webhooks. The access token expires every hour and must be refreshed.
TTL of 6 hours means the cache goes cold before next day's peak if the warmer runs too early — schedule the job 30-60 minutes before the expected traffic peak, not at midnight.

goal: Detect and alert on slow Postgres queries via GitLab issue creation
description: Agent inspects pg_stat_statements for queries slower than a threshold, deduplicates against recent alerts, and creates a GitLab issue assigned to the database team.

steps.action: Query pg_stat_statements for calls with mean_exec_time > 1000ms in the last hour

input_mapping.query: SELECT queryid, query, calls, mean_exec_time, total_exec_time FROM pg_stat_statements WHERE mean_exec_time > 1000 ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 20

steps.error_hint: pg_stat_statements must be enabled in postgresql.conf via shared_preload_libraries. Query text may be truncated to pg_stat_statements.track_activity_query_size.

steps.action: Search for existing open issues with label 'slow-query' and the same queryid to avoid duplicates
steps.error_hint: GitLab search is full-text — include the queryid as a stable token in issue bodies so dedup is reliable.

steps.action: Create new issues for slow queries that don't yet have an open tracker
steps.error_hint: Use project-scoped Project Access Tokens instead of user PATs for long-running agents — they're revocable per-project.

gotchas: pg_stat_statements must be loaded via shared_preload_libraries in postgresql.conf and requires a server restart to enable — it cannot be loaded dynamically. If not present, the query returns 'relation pg_stat_statements does not exist'.

GitLab issue search is full-text and fuzzy — deduplication by queryid requires embedding the queryid as a unique token (e.g., '[queryid:12345]') in the issue body so search reliably finds exact matches, not partial ones.

pg_stat_statements query text is truncated to track_activity_query_size (default 1024 bytes) — long queries like ORM-generated JOINs will be cut off, making root cause analysis harder. Increase this setting if you need full query text.

GitLab project access tokens have an expiration date (max 1 year) — long-running agents using these tokens will silently fail after expiry. Set a calendar reminder or build a token rotation mechanism.

goal: Sync Stripe payments to Xero accounting
description: When a Stripe payment succeeds, create a matching invoice and payment record in Xero for automated bookkeeping.

steps.action: Retrieve completed payment intent with customer and line item details
steps.error_hint: Amount is in cents (integer). Divide by 100 for Xero. Currency must be uppercase ISO 4217.

steps.action: Create or match contact in Xero by email, then create invoice and apply payment
steps.error_hint: Xero-tenant-id header is REQUIRED on every call. Amount must be decimal (not cents). Check fiscal period is open.

steps.action: Post payment sync confirmation to #accounting channel
steps.error_hint: Slack returns 200 OK even on failure — check ok:true in response body.

gotchas: Stripe amounts are cents (integer), Xero amounts are decimal — always divide by 100
Xero requires Xero-tenant-id header on every request — get it during OAuth flow
Multi-currency: Stripe and Xero must agree on currency code or reconciliation fails silently

goal: Stream Adyen payment data to BigQuery for analytics
description: Capture Adyen payment events and load them into BigQuery for dashboarding and fraud analysis.

steps.action: Fetch recent payment results from Adyen with payment method and risk scores
steps.error_hint: Amounts in minor units: {value:10000, currency:'USD'} = $100. Test URL vs live URL differ — check environment.

steps.action: Stream insert payment records into BigQuery payments table
steps.error_hint: Set useLegacySql:false. Streaming inserts have ~90 second delay before queryable. Schema must pre-exist.

steps.action: Post daily payment summary to #payments channel
steps.error_hint: Format amounts back to decimal for human readability in Slack message.

gotchas: Adyen minor units vary by currency (JPY has no decimals, EUR has 2) — use currency exponent table
BigQuery streaming insert buffer means data is not immediately queryable — don't chain a query right after insert

goal: Build RAG knowledge base from web content
description: Crawl target websites with Firecrawl, generate embeddings, and upsert into Pinecone for retrieval-augmented generation.

steps.action: Crawl target URL and return clean markdown pages
steps.error_hint: Crawl is async — returns job_id. Poll GET /crawl/{id} until status='completed'. Each page costs 1 credit.

steps.action: Generate embeddings for each crawled page using text-embedding-3-small
steps.error_hint: Max 8191 tokens per input. Split long pages into chunks of ~500 tokens with overlap. Model outputs 1536 dimensions.

steps.action: Upsert vectors with page content as metadata into Pinecone index
steps.error_hint: Index must be created with dimension=1536 matching embedding model. Use namespace for source separation. Api-Key header (not Bearer).

gotchas: Pinecone dimension MUST match embedding model: 1536 for text-embedding-3-small, 3072 for large
Firecrawl crawl is async — build a polling loop, don't assume immediate completion
Chunk strategy matters: 500 tokens with 50-token overlap is a good default for RAG

goal: Local RAG development with Firecrawl and Chroma
description: Scrape documentation sites and store in local Chroma for development-time RAG without cloud dependencies.

steps.action: Scrape target documentation pages to clean markdown
steps.error_hint: Use /scrape for single pages (sync), /crawl for full sites (async). formats=['markdown'] for LLM-ready output.

steps.action: Add documents to Chroma collection with auto-embedding
steps.error_hint: v2 API path: /tenants/{tenant}/databases/{db}/collections. NOT the old v1 /collections path. If embedding function configured, pass documents directly.

gotchas: Chroma v2 changed collection routing — use /tenants/default_tenant/databases/default_database/collections
Local Chroma runs with no auth by default — fine for dev but set CHROMA_SERVER_AUTHN_PROVIDER for production

goal: AI-powered competitive research pipeline
description: Use Tavily for broad search, Perplexity for deep analysis, and store findings in Notion.

steps.action: Search for recent information on target topic with AI-optimized results
steps.error_hint: Use search_depth='advanced' for deeper results (costs more credits). api_key goes in POST body, not header.

steps.action: Deep-dive analysis using Tavily findings as context, with web citations
steps.error_hint: Use model='sonar-pro' for best quality with citations. API requires payment method even for first use.

steps.action: Create research page in Notion database with analysis and sources
steps.error_hint: Notion API uses block-based content — convert markdown to Notion blocks. Max 100 blocks per request.

gotchas: Tavily api_key goes in POST body (not Authorization header) — non-standard
Perplexity citations array may contain URLs that are no longer accessible — cache key findings

goal: Automate hire-to-onboard pipeline
description: When a candidate is marked as hired in Greenhouse ATS, create their employee record in BambooHR and notify HR on Slack.

steps.action: Retrieve hired candidate details from Greenhouse Harvest API
steps.error_hint: Basic auth: API key as username, empty string as password. Per-page max 500 for bulk queries.

steps.action: Create employee record in BambooHR with hire details
steps.error_hint: URL requires subdomain (company-specific). Basic auth: API key as username, literal 'x' as password. Employee ID is in Location header, not body.

steps.action: Notify #hr-onboarding channel with new hire details
steps.error_hint: Check ok:true in response. Use blocks for rich formatting.

gotchas: Both Greenhouse and BambooHR use Basic auth but with different quirks: Greenhouse = key:'' (empty password), BambooHR = key:'x' (literal x)
BambooHR returns employee ID in Location header, not response body — parse the header
Employee fields are configurable per BambooHR account — use GET /meta/fields to check available fields first

goal: Convert support tickets to engineering tasks
description: When a Freshdesk ticket is tagged as a bug or feature request, create a corresponding ClickUp task and link back.

steps.action: Retrieve ticket details including description, priority, and customer info
steps.error_hint: Auth is API key with literal 'X' as password in Basic auth (same pattern as BambooHR). Priority is integer: 1=Low, 4=Urgent.

steps.action: Create task in engineering list with ticket context
steps.error_hint: Authorization: {api_key} — NO 'Bearer' prefix. Priority mapping differs: ClickUp uses 1=Urgent, 4=Low (reversed from Freshdesk).

steps.action: Add note to original ticket with ClickUp task link
steps.error_hint: Use private note (private:true) so customer doesn't see internal engineering link.

gotchas: Priority scales are REVERSED: Freshdesk 1=Low/4=Urgent, ClickUp 1=Urgent/4=Low — build a mapping
ClickUp Authorization header takes raw key without Bearer prefix — common 401 trap

goal: Enrich support tickets with order data
description: When a Gorgias ticket arrives, look up the customer's recent orders in BigCommerce and attach context to the ticket.

steps.action: Retrieve ticket and customer email from Gorgias
steps.error_hint: Basic auth: email as username, API key as password. Subdomain is account-specific.

steps.action: Search orders by customer email in BigCommerce
steps.error_hint: Two headers required: X-Auth-Token AND X-Auth-Client. Orders use v2 API path (/v2/orders), not v3.

steps.action: Add internal note with order summary to the ticket
steps.error_hint: Message type must be 'internal-note' to keep order details private from customer.

gotchas: BigCommerce orders endpoint is v2, not v3 — different base path than catalog endpoints
BigCommerce requires BOTH X-Auth-Token and X-Auth-Client headers — missing either gives 401

goal: CI/CD pipeline to Fly.io edge
description: On GitHub push, run tests via GitHub Actions, then deploy to Fly.io global edge network.

steps.action: Detect push event and trigger Actions workflow
steps.error_hint: Use fine-grained PAT with actions:write scope. Check .github/workflows/ exists.

steps.action: Wait for CI workflow to complete and check status
steps.error_hint: Poll GET /actions/runs/{id} — status goes queued→in_progress→completed. conclusion='success' means ready to deploy.

steps.action: Deploy to Fly.io using flyctl machine API if CI passed
steps.error_hint: Fly Machines API uses Bearer token from FLY_API_TOKEN. Base URL is api.machines.dev. Ensure app exists first.

gotchas: GitHub Actions workflow must complete before deploy — poll status, don't fire-and-forget
Fly.io Machines API is at api.machines.dev (not fly.io/api) — different domain

goal: CI/CD with CircleCI tests and Render deploy
description: GitHub push triggers CircleCI pipeline for testing, then deploys to Render on success.

steps.action: Detect push to main branch
steps.error_hint: Filter for branch=main to avoid deploying feature branches.

steps.action: Trigger pipeline and wait for workflow completion
steps.error_hint: CircleCI API v2 uses project slug format: gh/{org}/{repo}. Personal API token as Bearer.

steps.action: Trigger deploy on Render service
steps.error_hint: Render API key as Bearer token. Deploy is async — poll GET /services/{id}/deploys/{deployId} for completion.

gotchas: CircleCI project slug format is specific: gh/{org}/{repo} — not just the repo name
Render deploy is async — poll for completion before declaring success

goal: Sync contractor payments to accounting
description: When Deel processes a contractor payment, record the expense in Xero with proper categorization.

steps.action: Retrieve recent contractor invoices and payment details
steps.error_hint: Deel handles both EOR employees and contractors — filter by contract_type. Currency is per-contract (multi-currency).

steps.action: Create bill (accounts payable) entries in Xero for each contractor payment
steps.error_hint: Use Type='ACCPAY' for bills (not ACCREC which is receivables). Xero-tenant-id header required on every call.

steps.action: Post contractor payment summary to #finance channel
steps.error_hint: Check ok:true in Slack response.

gotchas: Deel is multi-currency by design — Xero bill currency must match the contract currency, not your base currency
Xero bill type is 'ACCPAY' (accounts payable), NOT 'ACCREC' (accounts receivable) — wrong type silently creates wrong entries

goal: Abandoned cart recovery via email automation
description: Detect abandoned carts in BigCommerce and trigger recovery email sequences in ActiveCampaign.

steps.action: Fetch abandoned carts older than 1 hour with customer details
steps.error_hint: Two headers: X-Auth-Token + X-Auth-Client. Abandoned carts are in v3: /carts?include=line_items.

steps.action: Create or update contacts and add to abandoned cart automation
steps.error_hint: Auth header is 'Api-Token' — NOT Authorization Bearer. Base URL is {account}.api-us1.com. Use POST /contact/sync for upsert.

goal: Generate monthly LLM cost reports from observability traces
description: Langfuse trace data is exported to BigQuery for cost aggregation, and a monthly summary is posted to Slack for budget tracking.

steps.action: Export Langfuse trace data via the API for the billing period
steps.error_hint: Langfuse API paginates large trace exports — implement cursor-based pagination to capture all traces in the period

steps.action: Insert traces and run cost aggregation query by model, project, and user
steps.error_hint: LLM pricing changes frequently — keep the pricing reference table updated or costs will be miscalculated

steps.action: Post the monthly LLM cost report to the finance/engineering channel
steps.error_hint: Format costs with currency symbols and 2 decimal places — raw floats are ambiguous

gotchas: Langfuse token counts may differ from provider billing — use Langfuse data for relative trends and provider invoices for absolute costs
BigQuery pricing table must account for different pricing tiers (e.g., input vs output tokens, cached vs non-cached)

goal: Automatically escalate support priority based on marketing lead scores
description: ActiveCampaign lead score changes trigger Freshdesk ticket priority updates and team alerts for high-value customers.

steps.action: Detect a lead score change event and retrieve the contact's current score and tier
steps.error_hint: ActiveCampaign webhooks fire on every score change — debounce to avoid processing rapid consecutive updates

steps.action: Update open ticket priorities for this customer based on their lead tier
steps.error_hint: Freshdesk priority values are integers (1=low to 4=urgent) — don't pass string labels

steps.action: Alert the support team lead about VIP customer ticket escalation
steps.error_hint: Only alert for tier upgrades to platinum/gold — alerting on every score change creates noise

gotchas: Lead score thresholds for tiers must match between ActiveCampaign automation rules and this recipe's tier mapping
Freshdesk ticket search by email may return tickets from different contacts at the same company domain — match on exact email, not domain

goal: Schedule and publish cross-platform social content with analytics tracking
description: Buffer schedules a post, which publishes to Twitter and YouTube Community, and engagement analytics are collected for reporting.

steps.action: Create and schedule a cross-platform social media post
steps.error_hint: Buffer profile_ids are specific to each connected account — verify IDs haven't changed after reconnecting accounts

steps.action: Fetch engagement metrics for the published tweet after a delay period
steps.error_hint: Twitter metrics API requires the tweet to be at least 15 minutes old — schedule the metrics fetch accordingly

steps.action: Fetch community post engagement metrics
steps.error_hint: YouTube Community posts API has limited metrics access — some engagement data may only be available via YouTube Studio

steps.action: Post the cross-platform analytics summary to the social team
steps.error_hint: Normalize metrics across platforms for fair comparison — Twitter impressions and YouTube views measure different things

gotchas: Buffer's update_id is not the same as the Twitter tweet_id — you need to fetch the published tweet ID from Buffer after posting
YouTube Community tab posts have limited API support — some operations may require scraping or manual verification

goal: Record enterprise payment captures in QuickBooks with team notification
description: Adyen captures a payment, QuickBooks records the journal entry for accounting, and Slack notifies the finance team.

steps.action: Capture the authorized payment and retrieve settlement details
steps.error_hint: Adyen capture requests can fail silently if the authorization has expired — check auth expiry before capturing

steps.action: Create a journal entry recording the payment in the general ledger
steps.error_hint: QuickBooks account IDs differ between sandbox and production — verify the account mapping after environment switches

steps.action: Notify the finance team about the recorded payment
steps.error_hint: Include the PSP reference in the Slack message so finance can cross-reference with the Adyen dashboard

gotchas: Adyen settlement amounts may differ from capture amounts due to fees — use the net settlement amount for the QuickBooks journal entry
Multi-currency payments require QuickBooks to have the currency enabled — unsupported currencies cause journal entry creation failure
